summary: Iteration 5 (Goodly Social Reach) testing complete. Backend: 20/20 pytest tests pass (~120s; real Claude sonnet-4-6 calls). Verified POST /api/social/audit for instagram/tiktok/youtube returns valid shape (overall_score 0-100, 6 categories, issues[], quick_wins[]). Verified POST /api/social/suggestions returns bio_rewrites/hashtag_sets/content_ideas/cta_examples for all 3 platforms. Verified POST /api/social/competitors returns overview/competitor_summaries/your_opportunities/content_gaps/quick_wins. Verified persistence via GET /api/social/audits with ?platform= filter. Verified 400 on invalid platform, 401/403 without auth. Verified _id is stripped on all responses. Frontend: landing hero updated to mention Google/Instagram/TikTok/YouTube + Done-for-you eyebrow, sidebar nav-social link present, qa-social dashboard card navigates to /app/social, social-root + all 3 platform tabs + all 9 mode buttons render, audit submit is correctly disabled until handle+niche are filled, suggestions form submitted and rendered sug-instagram-result successfully. Audit + competitor UI submissions failed mid-test due to EMERGENT_LLM_KEY BUDGET EXCEEDED (backend logs show 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 3.237, Max budget: 3.205') — NOT a code bug. Social.jsx already has toast.error on catch so UX surfaces failures correctly.

minor.issue: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget exceeded during UI testing (after pytest suite consumed allowance). Backend returns 502 with 'AI service error: ...' as designed. Main agent or environment owner needs to top up the key.

integration_issues.issue: When backend returns 502 (budget exceeded), the inline 'Analyzing...' submit button stays disabled until the toast is dismissed; the panel does NOT show an inline error state (only a toast). Consider also resetting the result panel placeholder to an error tile. Minor UX polish.

action_items: Top up EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget — currently exceeded (3.237 / 3.205). All /api/social/* AI calls 502 until refilled.
Optional polish: surface an inline error state in audit/competitor result panel in Social.jsx (currently only toast.error).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/social/audit — correctly validates platform via ALLOWED_PLATFORMS (returns 400 with sorted list), strips '@' from handle before storage and AI prompt, calls best-effort social_fetcher.fetch_profile_signals first then social_service.audit_profile, persists to db.social_audits with user_id+created_at index, excludes _id on return. Clean.

server.py /api/social/suggestions and /api/social/competitors — both gated by _check_platform, both wrap AI exceptions into HTTP 502 with informative detail. Suggestions also logs to ai_history. Good.
server.py GET /api/social/audits — supports optional ?platform= filter, sorted by created_at desc, limit 50, _id excluded. Verified history returns newly created audits and filter works.

social_service.py — _ask_json strips ```json fences and falls back to regex JSON extraction. PLATFORM_HINTS injects platform-specific best-practice knobs into the prompt. Prompts force EXACT JSON shape matching the API contract. SYSTEM message is platform-savvy. Clean.
social_service.py — _make_chat() raises clean RuntimeError when EMERGENT_LLM_KEY missing (currently exhausted-budget surfaces as ChatError from emergentintegrations which is caught upstream as 502).

Social.jsx — submit-disabled logic for audit is correctly tied to handle && niche (verified empty=disabled, handle-only=disabled, both=enabled). Suggestions and competitors have analogous gating. toast.error on all catch paths.
Social.jsx — bio rewrites and hashtag tags are clickable to copy via copy() helper (navigator.clipboard.writeText + toast.success). Good UX.
AppLayout.jsx + App.js — nav-social link uses Share2 icon between SEO Audit and AI Studio, route /app/social is registered. Verified rendering.
Dashboard.jsx — qa-projects replaced by qa-social; click navigates to /app/social. Verified.
Landing.jsx — hero h1 contains 'On Google, on Instagram, on TikTok, on YouTube. Your phone starts ringing.' and sub-eyebrow 'Done-for-you visibility for startups'. Verified in DOM.
PARALLEL request risk: All 3 endpoints make independent Claude calls. With current budget exhausted, every test/UI submit will 502. Not a code defect.

success_rate.frontend: 90% (UI structure + suggestions verified; audit + competitor UI submissions blocked by EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget exhaustion mid-test)

seed_data_creation: Tests created several real social_audits rows on demo user via real Claude calls (TEST_ prefix not used because audits have no business_name; entries are identifiable by handle prefixes 'acmepottery_*' and 'persist_test_handle'). Main agent can clean via db.social_audits.delete_many({handle: /(acmepottery_|persist_test_handle)/}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 5 endpoints (/api/social/audit, /suggestions, /competitors, GET /audits) verified end-to-end with real Claude calls. Budget exhaustion is the only failure mode observed in UI testing — pytest ran first and consumed the remaining budget. If retesting, expect 502s on /api/social/* until EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget is refilled. All structural assertions (auth gating, platform validation, response shape, persistence, _id exclusion, history filter) pass deterministically. Frontend Social.jsx already handles 502 via toast.error.
rca of the issue: EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget exhausted: backend logs show litellm.BadRequestError 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 3.23708725, Max budget: 3.204783559999999'. The 20 pytest tests + initial UI suggestion call consumed the remaining headroom; subsequent UI audit + competitor calls 502d. Mitigation: top up the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget. No code changes required.
